lint.detail: No errors. Warnings are dead_code in test stubs (expected for #[path] kernel module imports).

nonBlockingIssues.description: Section 19 (handle_send out-of-range) tests a trivially-true inline closure rather than using the shared validate_handle_nr function. Coverage already provided by Section 10.

adversarial_memory_share_overflow_pa test name implies it tests PA overflow but actually tests the page_count cap (2048) — the checked_add overflow path can never be reached because the cap is enforced first. Name is misleading but test is correct.
validate_interrupt_register returns Error::InvalidArgument for irq > u32::MAX, but kernel returns HandleError::InvalidHandle. Cosmetic mismatch — test verifies rejection behavior correctly.
State simulation helpers use string errors ('already started', 'wrong type') instead of kernel Error/HandleError enum variants. Internally consistent but diverges from kernel error semantics.
ProcessModel.thread_count and handle_count fields are initialized but never asserted on in any test. They add weight without verification value.

Vec-based channel/process/thread tables don't test slot reuse (slots grow monotonically). Timer and scheduling context tables DO test slot reuse. Minor coverage gap matching kernel's own Vec-based design.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in the kernel-auditor skill's Special Cases section that 'seeded RNG' guidance applies specifically to randomized stress/churn tests, not to deterministic boundary-value or state-confusion tests which are inherently deterministic.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature fuzz-boundaries-and-state worker noted that all boundary/state tests are deterministic by nature and the seeded RNG guidance didn't apply. The skill text currently says 'Use seeded RNG for any randomized tests' without distinguishing test types.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature description lists channel_create, scheduling_context_return, timer_create, interrupt_register, and thread_create as 'handle-accepting syscalls' but they don't accept handle arguments.
rejectedObservations.reason: Feature description accuracy is an orchestrator planning concern, not shared state. Worker correctly identified and handled the discrepancy.

rejectedObservations.observation: Stub module duplication across adversarial test files (pointers_handles, boundaries_state, churn all define similar stub modules).
rejectedObservations.reason: Follows established project pattern for test architecture. A shared utility module could reduce duplication at scale but is not actionable now and would change the established convention.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker transcript skeleton for fuzz-churn session shows 0 tool calls despite successful execution.
rejectedObservations.reason: Transcript infrastructure issue, not related to code quality or shared state.
